Title: Airborne Signals from a Wounded Leaf Facilitate Viral Spreading and Induce Antibacterial Resistance in Neighboring Plants

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: airborne wound signal that induces antibacterial resistance and alters plasmodesmata gating, indirectly facilitating viral spread
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: novel protein with repetitive amino acid sequence; no clear homolog reported
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: strongly induced by gaseous methanol; peak accumulation after methanol exposure and then attenuation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced in leaves of methanol-treated plants and in receiver plants exposed to wounded or PME-transgenic emitters
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: gaseous methanol exposure; neighboring wounded plants; neighboring PME-transgenic plants
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes plasmodesmata dilation/cell-to-cell transport and enhances TMV reproduction after methanol induction or ectopic expression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: SSH library identification; qRT-PCR; agroinjection/co-expression with 2xGFP movement assay; co-agroinjection with crTMV:GFP and GFP fluorimetry
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in pBin19 for transient expression
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: suppression subtractive hybridization (SSH) cDNA libraries; dbEST accession FN432041
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MIG-21 was identified as a methanol-inducible transcript in N. benthamiana. 2. Its mRNA accumulates strongly after methanol exposure and in receiver plants exposed to methanol-emitting neighbors. 3. Transient expression of MIG-21 increases 2xGFP cell-to-cell movement and enhances crTMV:GFP accumulation, linking methanol response to viral spread.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: previously unidentified gene in this paper; implicated in plasmodesmata gating but precise molecular mechanism remains unknown
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: enhanced viral spread/TMV reproduction despite association with antibacterial defense
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: limited direct nutritional value; cautions against methanol-responsive manipulation because improved antibacterial resistance may increase viral susceptibility

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Nicotiana benthamiana endogenous PME; also fungal PME transgene in transgenic tobacco background discussed
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: PME-transgenic tobacco line pro1 for some experiments
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: pectin demethylation/cell wall methylester metabolism leading to methanol emission
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream/direct source step for methanol release
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: removes methyl esters from pectin in the cell wall, releasing methanol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as key control step for wound-induced methanol emission
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: airborne signal inducing antibacterial resistance in neighboring plants; also linked to increased plasmodesmata gating and viral spread
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: 1.7-kb transcript undetectable in intact leaves, induced 1 h after Celite rubbing and increased by 8 h; also increased after TMV inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: elevated in wounded N. benthamiana leaves and constitutively high in PME-transgenic tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: leaf wounding by Celite rubbing; TMV inoculation; GLV cis-3-hexen-1-ol can stimulate PME-related methanol emission
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: increased PME expression/activity elevates methanol emission, which induces antibacterial resistance in neighboring plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA gel blot/Northern blot; methanol headspace quantification in water-drop and flow-through systems; transgenic overexpression in tobacco; bacterial growth assays in receivers
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: constitutive transgene expression in PME-transgenic tobacco line pro1; promoter details not reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Wounding induces de novo PME transcript accumulation in N. benthamiana leaves. 2. Wounding and PME overexpression correlate with strongly increased gaseous methanol emission. 3. Airborne methanol from PME-overexpressing plants is sufficient to induce antibacterial resistance in neighboring plants.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: paper centers on defense signaling rather than nutrient metabolism; methanol is treated as a wound-induced volatile metabolite. PME itself was not induced by methanol and therefore is not a MIG.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: enhanced methanol signaling facilitates TMV spread in neighboring plants; PME-transgenic tobacco also previously noted to show dwarf phenotype and altered cell wall metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: could be used to prime antibacterial resistance via volatile signaling, but deployment must consider increased viral spread risk and plant growth/cell-wall tradeoffs

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: callose/β-1,3-glucan turnover associated with plasmodesmata gating and defense
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream effector step responding to methanol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for remodeling that affects methanol-associated defense/viral spread phenotype rather than directly producing methanol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydrolyzes β-1,3-glucan, consistent with callose turnover and increased plasmodesmata permeability
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: methanol serves as inducer of BG expression, linking airborne signaling to defense and viral movement phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: pathogenesis-related PR-2 β-1,3-glucanase, including vacuolar/basic isoforms
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: strongly methanol inducible; up to ~400-fold mRNA increase after 18 h methanol treatment; prolonged elevated expression up to 5 days after treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highly induced in methanol-treated leaves and in receiver plants exposed to wounded or PME-transgenic emitters
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: gaseous methanol exposure; neighboring wounded plants; neighboring PME-transgenic plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enhances plasmodesmata gating/cell-to-cell movement and promotes TMV reproduction when expressed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: SSH identification; qRT-PCR; transient co-agroinjection with 2xGFP movement assay; co-agroinjection with crTMV:GFP and fluorimetric GFP analysis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in pBin19 for transient expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BG is one of the most strongly methanol-inducible genes identified in N. benthamiana. 2. BG transcript accumulation is sustained after methanol exposure and is induced in neighboring receiver plants. 3. BG expression increases 2xGFP movement and enhances TMV-derived GFP accumulation, indicating a role in plasmodesmata opening and viral spread.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: selected as one of the most abundant MIGs for mechanistic follow-up; contributes to defense-associated but virus-favoring cell-to-cell communication
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: greater viral spread/TMV reproduction associated with increased plasmodesmata gating
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: not directly useful for nutrient trait engineering; manipulation should be cautious because defense-associated expression can promote virus movement

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: airborne wound signal that induces defense-related gene expression and cell-to-cell communication changes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly inducible by methanol; nearly 50-fold increase after 18 h and strongest relative accumulation among examined MIGs at 6 h
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced in leaves of methanol-treated plants and in receiver plants exposed to wounded or PME-transgenic emitters
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: gaseous methanol exposure; neighboring wounded plants; neighboring PME-transgenic plants
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: cell-to-cell trafficking/plasmodesmata gating in methanol-triggered defense context
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: airborne methanol from wounded or PME-overexpressing plants
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive regulation of plasmodesmata gating/cell-to-cell communication
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-dependent controller linking methanol signaling to increased intercellular transport and viral spread
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes cell-to-cell movement and enhances TMV reproduction after methanol induction or transient expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: SSH identification; qRT-PCR; transient co-agroinjection with 2xGFP movement assay; co-agroinjection with crTMV:GFP and GFP fluorimetry
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in pBin19 for transient expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NCAPP is a methanol-inducible gene strongly upregulated in methanol-treated N. benthamiana and in receiver plants near methanol emitters. 2. NCAPP transient expression increases 2xGFP movement, indicating enhanced plasmodesmata gating. 3. NCAPP co-expression promotes crTMV:GFP accumulation, linking methanol-induced trafficking regulation to viral reproduction.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: although represented by only three SSH clones, qRT-PCR showed NCAPP is among the most strongly inducible MIGs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: not suitable for nutritional engineering; any use in defense priming must consider virus susceptibility risk
